title: RFE has rfe-creator-needs-attention
symptom: RFE failed the quality rubric or review flagged issues.
steps: Open the RHAIRFE in Jira and read the review feedback (ask engineering for the HTML review report if needed).
Fix failing criteria — usually WHY (business justification), Right-sized (split RFEs), or Open to HOW (avoid prescribing architecture).
If the RFE is too large, split into separate tickets.
Ask AI Engineering to re-run the rfe-creator review on your ticket.
Confirm rfe-creator-autofix-rubric-pass or tech-reviewed appears before adding strat scope labels.

title: RFE has feasibility-fail or feasibility-unknown
symptom: The need may not be achievable on the current platform, or architecture context was insufficient.
steps: Engage the component owner or staff engineer listed on related STRATs.
Determine whether the RFE needs reframing, deferral, or an architecture overlay update.
If scope changes, update the RFE and return to quality review.
If architecture context was wrong, engineering submits an overlay PR to architecture-context.

title: RFE passed quality but has no strat-creator scope
symptom: RFE has quality pass but no STRAT has been created.
steps: Add strat-creator-3.5 or strat-creator-3.6 label, or set a matching Target Version for your release.
Confirm status is not Draft, Closed, or Resolved.
If all gates pass, wait for strat-creator CI pickup (see §4).

title: RFE has scope + quality but no STRAT yet
symptom: Everything looks ready on the RFE, but no linked RHAISTRAT exists.
owner: Wait on CI; PM escalates if > ~1 week
steps: Verify no existing RHAISTRAT is already linked (Cloners link).
Check the strat dashboard for recent batch runs.
Confirm the RFE is not stuck behind batch limits (default 10 per run).
Escalate to AI Engineering if eligible for multiple CI cycles without pickup.
jqlTemplate: project = RHAIRFE AND labels in ("strat-creator-3.6", "strat-creator-3.5") AND labels in ("rfe-creator-autofix-rubric-pass", "tech-reviewed") AND status not in (Closed, Resolved, Draft)

title: STRAT has strat-creator-needs-attention
symptom: CI scored the strategy below the approval threshold.
steps: Staff engineer pulls the strategy and reads the review score table.
Fix inputs (overlay, Staff Engineer / SME Input, or RFE update) — not the AI Strategy section.
Re-run refine and review until scores improve.
Push back to CI and wait for strat-creator-rubric-pass.
PM: provide business justification or scope clarifications when flagged.

title: STRAT has rubric-pass but no human-sign-off
symptom: CI approved the strategy; waiting on human sign-off.
steps: Staff engineer reviews scores, dependencies, effort, and test criteria.
Make minor improvements via Staff Engineer / SME Input if needed.
Sign off via strat-creator workflow — applies strat-creator-human-sign-off.
PM: escalate to the staff engineer or architect for your component.
jqlTemplate: project = RHAISTRAT AND labels = "strat-creator-rubric-pass" AND labels not in ("strat-creator-human-sign-off")

title: STRAT has human-sign-off but not planning-ready
symptom: Strategy approved but feature fails one or more readiness gates.
steps: Confirm strat-creator-human-sign-off is present.
Assign Product Manager and delivery owner on the RHAISTRAT ticket.
Advance status past New / Refinement.
Set Target Version for your release.
Populate RICE scores (Reach, Impact, Confidence, Effort).
Fix any hygiene issues (assignee, fix version) — check Org Pulse Feature Readiness.
jqlTemplate: project = RHAISTRAT AND labels = "strat-creator-human-sign-off" AND status in (New, Refinement)

title: Planning-ready but missing rp-qg1-pass
symptom: Feature passes readiness but lacks the planning quality gate label.
steps: Confirm all §7 checklist items pass, especially RICE scores.
Verify fix version / target version align with the release.
Ask the planning owner to run Release Quality Gate 1 for your release.
Passing features receive rp-qg1-pass (3.5).
jqlTemplate: project = RHAISTRAT AND labels = "strat-creator-human-sign-off" AND labels not in ("rp-qg1-pass") AND status != Closed

title: Feature has rp-qg1-fail
symptom: Feature failed Release Quality Gate 1 (Definition of Ready).
steps: Open Feature Readiness in Org Pulse to see which DoR checks failed.
Fix blockers on the RHAISTRAT ticket (owners, RICE, status).
Request re-evaluation through the release planning process.

title: Feature passed Gate 1 but wrong phase or deferred
symptom: Strategy evaluation assigned unexpected phase or deferral.
steps: Read the strategy evaluation rationale in the Gate 1 report.
If Big Rock mapping is wrong, request a Gate 1 adjustment.
If deferral is capacity-driven, request a Gate 2 adjustment.
Document business justification for the planning review.

title: Gate 2 approved but no child epics
symptom: Feature is in the committed plan but has no RHAI child epics.
steps: Confirm Gate 2 approval and epic manifest exist (Org Pulse Release Plan).
Verify strat-creator-human-sign-off and complete strategy content.
Check whether epic-creator CI has run for your release batch.
Ask AI Engineering to run epic decomposition for your RHAISTRAT key.
jqlTemplate: project = RHAISTRAT AND labels = "strat-creator-human-sign-off" AND labels not in ("epic-creator-auto-decomposed") AND status != Closed

title: Decomposition review failed
symptom: epic-creator ran but epics were not submitted (review pass: false).
owner: Staff engineer; PM if scope gaps
steps: Engineering reads the decomp review artifact for criterion scores.
Fix missing HLR coverage, DAG edges, or Investigation vs Implementation issues.
Re-run decomposition until review passes (≥ 10/14, no zero criteria).
PM: update Staff Engineer / SME Input or source RFE if scope is ambiguous.
